_patch_meta.description: C10 DimReview R077 honesty — 6 group dimension assignments + registry stamp + 1 SD pointer. All automated labels confirmed on dimension; dominant_subject set throughout. Manual application required.

operations.description: 3246-001 H6662 tsaddiq (constitutive inner moral orientation) — Moral Character / HUMAN.
3246-002 H6662 tsaddiq (God tests and vindicates the righteous) — Moral Character / GOD. Dominant subject is GOD as moral assessor.
3246-003 H6662 tsaddiq (righteous person rejoices, takes refuge) — Emotion-Positive / HUMAN.
911-001 H6666 tsedeqah (inner moral character in just conduct) — Moral Character / HUMAN.
911-002 H6666 tsedeqah (righteousness credited/vindicated by God, including imputation) — Moral Character / GOD. GOD as the active creditor.
911-003 H6666 tsedeqah (joy and exultation at received righteousness) — Emotion-Positive / HUMAN.

record.description: R077 (tsaddiq/tsedeqah vocabulary) shows that Hebrew righteousness operates across two distinct inner-being registers in the same verse corpus: (1) Moral Character — the stable quality of the righteous person and the standard God applies; (2) Emotion-Positive — the joyful, responsive orientation of the person who receives righteousness as gift. This internal split is analytically significant for Session D: righteousness is simultaneously a moral constitution and an affective posture. The asymmetry of dominant subjects (GOD as assessor/creditor; HUMAN as bearer and responder) should be noted in any Session D synthesis of the righteousness concept.

operations.description: SD pointer DIM-77-SD002 — righteousness as dual register: moral constitution and affective posture.
Registry stamp — R077 honesty DimReview Complete.
